name: Zorya Vechernyaya
description: Zorya Vechernyaya, also known as the Evening Star, is one of the two or three Zoryas, the dawn goddesses in Slavic mythology. She is the sister (or sometimes, wife) of Zorya Utrennyaya, the Morning Star. Zorya Vechernyaya's primary role is to open the western gates of the celestial palace each evening for the sun chariot as it returns from its daily journey across the sky. After opening the gates, she ensures the sun's safe passage and prepares for its rest. She is often depicted as a guardian of the sun, protecting it from harm during its nightly journey through the underworld. Zorya Vechernyaya is associated with twilight, evening, rest, and protection. She is sometimes believed to be a protector of travelers and a bringer of peace at the end of the day. Unlike Zorya Utrennyaya, who heralds new beginnings and activity, Zorya Vechernyaya symbolizes the closure of the day and the transition to night. Some interpretations suggest she may be associated with the moon as well, further linking her to the nighttime realm. While not as prominently featured in surviving folklore as her sister, Zorya Vechernyaya remains an important figure in the Slavic understanding of the cosmos and the daily cycle of light and darkness. Her watchful presence ensures the balance of the world and the sun's eventual return.
subtitle: Evening Star, Guardian of Twilight

keyMyths.title: The Opening of the Western Gates
keyMyths.description: Each evening, Zorya Vechernyaya opens the western gates of the heavens to allow the sun to return. She ensures its safe passage as it travels through the underworld.

keyMyths.title: Sister of the Morning Star
keyMyths.description: Zorya Vechernyaya is the sister of Zorya Utrennyaya, representing the opposite ends of the day. Together they guard the sun's journey.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the Sun
keyMyths.description: Zorya Vechernyaya protects the sun from harm during its nightly journey through the underworld. She ensures its safe return at dawn.

sources.author: Afanasyev, A. N.
sources.work: Poetic Outlook on Nature by the Slavs
sources.description: A comprehensive work on Slavic folklore and mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive work on Slavic folklore and mythology.
